title: Can store music change which products people pick? — Daniel Fox
meta_description: Through musical priming, the music overhead activates cultural associations that steer purchases below conscious awareness. What the research shows about how music changes which products people choose.
schema: <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","headline":"Can store music change which products people pick?","description":"How musical priming steers product choice toward congruent categories, and why awareness doesn't cancel the effect.","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox","alternateName":"Daniel Fox","jobTitle":"Retail Music Behaviorist","url":"https://danielchristopherfox.com"},"publisher":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox"},"mainEntityOfPage":"https://danielchristopherfox.com/notes/can-music-change-what-people-buy.html","about":["Retail music","Musical priming","Product choice","Consumer behavior"]}</script>
